title: LME Aluminium Price Surge Hits $3,269/Tonne in March 2026
summary: LME aluminium prices reached $3,269 per tonne on March 3, 2026, driven by inventory drawdowns and supply constraints. Live warrants dropped 10.77%, signaling accelerated consumption, while the forward curve shows near-term strength but long-term normalization. Regional energy transition challenges further compound supply tightness.

title: The Silver Squeeze: Aluminum Surges Past $3000 as 2026 Supply Crunch Hits Global Markets
summary: As of March 23, 2026, LME aluminum spot prices hit $3,220 per tonne amid supply deficits, China's production caps, and surging demand from EVs and renewables. The EU's CBAM has created a green premium of $150 per tonne, benefiting low-carbon producers like Alcoa and Rio Tinto. Downstream industries face sustained high costs.

title: Aluminium Prices Soar as Tariffs and Energy Costs Reshape the Market
summary: Aluminum futures on the LME exceeded levels last seen in April 2022, part of a base metals rally fueled by tariffs and energy costs. Goldman Sachs raised its H1 2026 forecast to $3,150 per ton from $2,575, though still below current prices. Bullish sentiment continues amid supply tightness.

title: U.S. aluminum prices hit record highs amid tariffs, low supply
summary: U.S. aluminum prices surged 40% to over $5,200 per metric ton after tariffs doubled to 50%, with Midwest premiums hitting a record 96 cents per pound. Inventories fell sharply, compounded by global LME price rises and supply shortages. Higher costs threaten manufacturers and may fuel inflation.

title: Aluminum Prices Soar to Four-Year High, Creating a Divide in U.S. Industry
summary: Aluminum prices reached a four-year high, benefiting U.S. producers like Alcoa and Century Aluminum amid global price surges and regional premiums. Goldman Sachs revised its H1 2026 average price forecast upward to $3,150 per ton. The rally highlights a divide between producers and cost-sensitive industries.

title: Aluminium reaches four-year highs due to Middle East shipping disruptions
summary: Aluminum prices hit four-year highs as Middle East conflict closed the Strait of Hormuz, disrupting shipments from the region. China's primary aluminum output increased 3% in January-February 2026 due to higher profit margins, with production reaching 7.53 million metric tonnes. The conflict has virtually halted aluminum exports from a region accounting for about 9% of global production.

title: The Silver Squeeze: Aluminum Surges Past $3,000 as 2026 Supply Crunch Hits Global Markets
summary: Aluminum prices breached $3,000 per tonne as of March 23, 2026, driven by China's shift to net importer status due to its capacity-for-capacity replacement policy and surging demand from EV and solar industries. The market is experiencing unprecedented volatility with a structural deficit, prompting companies to explore near-shoring, vertical integration, and circular economy solutions including recycling infrastructure expansion.

title: Supply fears lift LME aluminium to nearly four-year high
summary: London Metal Exchange aluminum prices hit their highest level in nearly four years at $3,544 per metric ton, driven by Middle East conflict concerns and supply disruptions. Qatalum in Qatar began shutting down production due to natural gas shortages, and Aluminium Bahrain declared force majeure on shipments, exacerbating global supply tightness.

title: Aluminium prices expected to rise with prolonged war set to hit Gulf producers
summary: Aluminum prices are expected to remain elevated as the Strait of Hormuz closure continues to disrupt Middle East exports, with prices potentially reaching $3,700-$4,000 per tonne if disruptions persist. Gulf producers have approximately three to four weeks of raw materials on site to continue operations before supply becomes critically constrained.

title: Aluminium Prices Soar as Tariffs and Energy Costs Reshape the Market
summary: Aluminum prices jumped to nearly four-year highs above $3,000 per tonne, with U.S. premiums reaching historical peaks. Middle Eastern producers with low-cost power are expanding capacity through recycling and investments, while U.S. downstream industries face months or years of supply constraints despite restart efforts at facilities like Century Aluminum's Mt. Holly plant.

title: Conglin Aluminum Technology Expands Global Aluminum Solutions with Advanced Manufacturing and Precision Engineering
summary: Conglin Aluminum Technology has expanded its capabilities in aluminum extrusion, precision machining, and customized product development to meet growing demand for lightweight, durable, and sustainable materials. The company delivers aluminum components with excellent strength-to-weight ratios and superior corrosion resistance, with applications including over 260 aluminum bridge structures and components for thousands of rail vehicles. The company actively promotes sustainable manufacturing practices, with aluminum components reducing vehicle weight by up to 400 kilograms compared to steel alternatives.

title: ALUMINIUM 2026 Launches New Industry Innovation Awards
summary: ALUMINIUM 2026 has launched new Industry Innovation Awards celebrating breakthroughs in sustainable manufacturing, technology, and smart automation across three key categories. The awards will recognize implemented solutions, pilot projects, prototypes, and validated innovations brought to market within the past 48 months, with submissions opening in May and winners announced at the event.

title: The Aluminum Renaissance: A Deep-Dive into Alcoa's (AA) 2026 Strategic Pivot
summary: Alcoa is advancing its competitive position through its Sustana line of low-carbon products, including EcoSource (the world's first low-carbon alumina) and EcoLum (aluminum produced with less than 4.0 kg CO2e per kg). The company's ELYSIS joint venture with Rio Tinto has moved into extended validation phase with carbon-free smelting technology that emits pure oxygen instead of CO2, positioning Alcoa as a future technology licensor for the global smelting industry.

title: Aluminium Industry in 2026: Global Trends, Innovations, and What Buyers Should Expect
summary: Aluminum has become a strategic material driving global manufacturing and clean energy transition, with 100 percent recyclability and no loss of properties. Leading manufacturers are adopting advanced die manufacturing, precision billet casting, AI-enabled process monitoring, and real-time extrusion parameter optimization to improve quality and reduce production variability.

title: Why Is Aluminum Scrap Suddenly Shaping the Future? The 2026 Global Shockwave Explained
summary: The aluminum scrap industry is undergoing a circular economy revolution, with companies investing in closed-loop recycling, green smelting technologies, and advanced sorting systems. Recycled aluminum uses up to 95% less energy than primary production, and demand is booming in Asia-Pacific, with China sourcing about 60% of its building-sector aluminum from recycled materials.

title: US ALUMINUM: Midstream leaders look to data center boom as EV trend falters
summary: US midstream aluminum manufacturers are shifting focus from electric vehicles to data center construction, driven by AI infrastructure demand. EV and solar-related aluminum demand is expected to drop in 2026 following the end of US incentives, while data center investments are seen as a strategic necessity.

title: Commodity Price Watch: March 2026
summary: Steel prices are higher in the US and Europe due to shipping disruptions and higher electricity costs, while remaining little changed in most of Asia amid excess output. Demand is flat to negative across key regions like Europe, the US, and China, exacerbated by the ongoing Middle East war. US mills maintain pricing power as imports are restricted.

title: US flat steel pricing continues climb, lower March scrap could be balanced by low steel imports
summary: US flat steel prices, including hot-rolled coils, continued rising with a $10/nt increase last week, up nearly 9% since early 2026. Lower March scrap prices may be offset by reduced steel imports. Related markets show firmness in Brazilian slab exports and ex-Asia FOB slab prices.

title: Metal Price News - March
summary: Stainless steel prices are expected to rise due to increasing oil prices impacting production and freight costs, with potential nickel price increases if the Middle East war prolongs. European production benefits from CBAM and quotas shifting supply locally, but bottlenecks may drive prices higher. Base prices for bars continue upward amid limited Asian imports.

title: Q1 2026 Metal Pricing Trends: What Contractors Must Know
summary: Steel market shows rising mill output and strong demand into Q1 2026, contributing to upward pricing trends. Tariffs, global events, and supply chain issues add volatility, with no relief expected soon. Contractors are advised to use escalation clauses amid capacity constraints and longer lead times as construction season approaches.

title: Market Update: March 2026
summary: Domestic steel pricing continues climbing above $1,000 per ton. Trade restrictions limit North American supply outside the US, supporting higher prices. Ongoing factors sustain the upward market trend.

title: Auction Scheduled for Alton Steel Equipment
summary: The production assets of Alton Steel, an Illinois-based special bar quality manufacturer that shut down suddenly in January, are to be sold off through a large, online auction scheduled for next month. This development signals the end of operations at the facility, impacting local steel production capacity.

title: Joint venture ArcelorMittal Nippon Steel India (AM/NS India) held a foundation stone–laying ceremony for its new integrated steel plant
summary: ArcelorMittal Nippon Steel India held a foundation stone-laying ceremony for its new integrated steel plant, marking a significant step toward expanding production capacity in India. The project aims to boost regional steel output through advanced facilities.

title: Copper - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Copper fell to 5.38 USD/Lbs on March 24, 2026, down 1.14% from the previous day. Over the past month, prices have declined 10.15%, though still up 3.37% year-over-year. Earlier on March 23, copper hit a 14-week low of 5.27 USD/Lbs, down 2% to 5.3246 USD/Lbs amid ongoing market weakness.

title: Copper Drops $0.19 | Scrap Metal Prices Mixed — March 19, 2026
summary: Copper led the scrap metal market decline, falling $0.19 per pound across major grades on March 19, 2026, while other metals held steady. Comex copper settled at $5.48 per pound, down 4.9%, and LME 3-month copper closed at $12,395.50 per ton, down 2.97%. The drop aligns with macro pressures from a stronger US dollar and demand concerns tied to energy costs and geopolitical disruptions.

title: Copper Scrap Scrap Prices – as of March 24, 2026
summary: Copper scrap prices are listed at $3.96/lb as of March 24, 2026, with variations by type such as Light Copper at $3.37/lb and Enameled Copper at $2.35/lb. These prices reflect current market conditions for scrap yards. The data provides insights into secondary market trends amid primary copper price declines.

title: An immediate transition to copper is technically and economically feasible
summary: With rising silver prices, large solar manufacturers are switching to copper for cell metallization, expected to be followed industry-wide. Experts from ISC Konstanz and UNSW indicate that copper offers significant cost savings with minimal efficiency trade-offs and no new reliability risks when properly implemented. Silver will still be used in small amounts for specific functions like diffusion barriers.

title: Ten major mining tech trends in 2026: Part 1
summary: Copper companies like Freeport-McMoRan are advancing sustainability through reprocessing waste stockpiles to produce up to 360,000 tons of copper annually using new leaching technologies. Amerigo generates revenue from waste at El Teniente mine, equivalent to a 30,000-tonne copper mine. Innovations also target reducing water consumption in copper processing by up to 78% via advanced flowsheets.

title: Energy Innovation & Sustainability News
summary: Clean energy company Copper secured a $32 million contract to develop 10,000 battery-equipped induction stoves for New York public housing, advancing sustainable domestic supply chains for critical battery materials. The update highlights trends in battery technologies for electrification amid growing energy demands and evolving regulations. Manufacturing supply chains for critical minerals are reshoring to the U.S. to meet demand.

title: Gunnison Copper COO on 2026 Copper Production Goals
summary: Gunnison Copper aims for two million pounds of monthly production using innovative oxide and Tom leach systems, partnering with Rio Tinto's technology division and Amazon Web Services for U.S. supply chain integration into data centers. The company has reduced debt to reinvest in operations, targeting major growth in 2026 with an upcoming updated Resource Assessment. COO Robert Winton emphasizes breaking trends in copper technologies.

title: FLASH: Indonesia's ESDM confirms plan to reduce nickel production to 250-260 million tonnes for 2026
summary: Indonesia's Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources (ESDM) has confirmed a nickel production target of 250-260 million tonnes for 2026, down from 379 million tonnes in 2025, to align with domestic smelting capacity. The reduction aims to support nickel prices, which surged to US$18,000/tonne following the announcement. The government is also requiring large smelters to source from local permit holders to prevent monopolies.

title: Nickel Market Faces Structural Shift as Top Producers Coordinate
summary: The nickel market is shifting as Indonesia and the Philippines coordinate supply, driving prices above $18,000 per ton. Indonesia slashed Eramet's ore quota from 42 million to 12 million tons, removing about 300,000 tons of nickel supply. Physical indicators and disruptions like Sherritt's curtailments in Cuba support sustained higher prices.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output
summary: PT Weda Bay Nickel, operating the world's biggest nickel mine in Indonesia, must cut ore production quota to 12 million tons from 42 million tons to boost prices. The company plans to seek a revision. Nickel prices rose last month after Indonesia announced output cuts.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel prices fell to 16,857.75 USD/T on March 24, 2026, amid supply concerns from Indonesia's 2026 ore quota of 260-270 million wet metric tons, constraining smelter utilization to 70-75%. Indonesian refiners face potential cuts due to Middle Eastern sulfur disruptions. Chinese steel mills' demand provides some support.

title: Indonesia cuts 2026 nickel quota, global prices jump immediately
summary: Indonesia has reduced its 2026 nickel quota, sparking immediate global price increases due to supply concerns. The cut aligns with efforts to manage production and support market prices.

title: Inside a $1.1B deal to reshore critical minerals refining
summary: Nth Cycle has secured a $1.1 billion agreement with Trafigura to expand its electrochemical refining system for nickel, cobalt, copper, and other critical minerals from scrap and black mass in Ohio. The technology enables smaller, more cost-effective facilities compared to traditional refineries, addressing U.S. reliance on Indonesia and China for nickel processing. This deal supports reshoring efforts amid geopolitical tensions and promotes sustainable recycling of battery materials.

title: Nickel-zinc batteries considered the new data centre gold standard
summary: Nickel-zinc batteries are emerging as a gold standard for data centers strained by AI demands, offering high power density and sustainability advantages. The article highlights their role in balancing performance with environmental goals as data centers expand. This innovation supports efficient, eco-friendly power solutions for high-intensity computing.

title: Nickel-zinc and the next era of sustainable data centre power in Europe
summary: As AI and cloud computing grow in Europe, nickel-zinc batteries are positioned to deliver sustainable, high-density power for data centers facing sustainability pressures. The technology addresses the need for efficient energy storage amid rising power demands. It represents a shift toward greener infrastructure in the region.
